name: Oprocesso

values.name: actionbox
values.comment: 'actionbox' is where one sends actions to, when using the provided framework.

values.name: async
values.comment: An asynchronous modification of the model is any task which gets invoked based on the current model and returns with a modification. One can use 'async' to lift such functions.
values.type: (model -> Task.Task error (model -> model)) -> Action error model

values.name: asyncOn
values.comment:
Often the invocation of tasks depends on a certain value out of the model which can be reached by a getter. 'asyncOn' is meant to be used in such a situation:

  requestJson `asyncOn` .typed
values.type: (a -> Task.Task error (model -> model)) -> (model -> a) -> Action error model

values.name: hook
values.comment:
'hook' sets up the main model signal.

  import Oprocesso  as O
  main = Signal.map view (O.hook initmodel)

values.name: invoke
values.comment: 'invoke' is the direct way to invoke an action.

values.name: ioport
values.comment:
'ioport' sets up a port which runs the asynchronous tasks and calls the actions by feeding them back into the 'actionbox'.

  import Oprocesso as O

  port asyncrunner : Signal (Task x ())
  port asyncrunner = O.ioport initmodel

values.name: mmstack
values.comment: The 'mmstack' is a signal consisting of the latest model and the next action, if there is any. It *forks* the actionbox by past folding pure operations and keeping track of the latest next action.
values.type: model -> Signal.Signal ( model, Maybe (RepType error model) )

values.name: next
values.comment: 'next' is the asynchronous combinator. It combines two actions such that both get invoked asynchronously.
values.type: Action error model -> Action error model -> Action error model

values.name: onfail
values.comment:
'onfail' is the error handling combinator. Its second argument is an error handler; on none asynchronous actions, nothing changes.


   (requestJson `asyncOn` .typed)
        `onfail` \err -> pure <| addEntry <| "Error happened: " ++ err
values.type: Action error model -> (error -> Action x model) -> Action x model

values.name: pure
values.comment:
The easiest building blocks are actions which just change the model, these are so called 'pure' ones.

  pure (\m -> { m | entries <- asEntry m.typed
                  , typed   <- ""})

values.name: pureParam
values.comment:
Usually, a model gets modified based on a parameter. These can be lifted with `pureParam`:

  addString : String -> Model -> Model
  addString s = \m -> { m | entries <- asEntry s }

  pureParam addString "an Entry"
values.type: (a -> model -> model) -> a -> Action error model

values.name: task
values.comment: An asynchronous modification which does not depend on the current model can be lifted with 'task'.
values.type: Task.Task error (model -> model) -> Action error model

values.name: thenDo
values.comment: 'thenDo' can be thought of as an `onSuccess` combinator: it combines two actions `act1` and `act2` in such a way, that if `act1` succeeds (which is always the case if no asynchronous action is involved) then right after it returns, `act2` takes place.
values.type: Action error model -> Action error model -> Action error model

name: Oprocesso.EDSL
Oprocesso.Types

values.name: andThen
values.type: State s a -> (a -> State s b) -> State s b

values.name: mapState
values.type: (s -> s) -> State s a -> State s a

values.name: run
values.type: State s a -> s -> ( s, a )
